name: Ken Griffey Jr. Statue
summary: Bronze statue by Lou Cella (2017). Part of statue row with Edgar Martinez (2021) and Ichiro Suzuki (April 2026).

name: "The Tempest" Chandelier
summary: Chandelier of 1,000 resin baseball bats above the home plate entry. Part of extensive public art program.

name: Ichiro Suzuki Statue
summary: Newest statue, unveiled April 10, 2026 alongside Ichiro's #51 retirement ceremony.

name: The T-Mobile 'Pen
summary: Standing-room social zone with fire pits, bars, and celebrity chef food (Ethan Stowell). Adjacent to both bullpens. Happy hour starts at gate opening.
research: STUDENT RESEARCH GOES HERE: Atmosphere analysis, demographic observations, spending patterns, engagement metrics.

name: Kids Play Area & Moose's Den
summary: Play zone, Moose mascot meet-and-greet, Kids Clubhouse retail. Free Kids Club membership includes game ticket.
research: STUDENT RESEARCH GOES HERE: Family engagement strategy analysis.

name: Left Field Bleachers
summary: Most affordable seating. Metal benches, die-hard fan territory. Catwalk shortcut above avoids main concourse congestion.

name: Walk-Off Market
summary: MLB's first cashierless store (May 2022), Amazon Just Walk Out technology. Expanded to four locations.
research: STUDENT RESEARCH GOES HERE: Fan adoption, transaction speed, revenue impact.

name: Mariners Hall of Fame
summary: Co-located with Baseball Museum of the Pacific Northwest. 11 inductees. Interactive exhibits covering PNW baseball from the 1800s.

name: Diamond Club
summary: Most exclusive seating. All-inclusive food/beverages, private entrance, in-seat wait service, climate-controlled lounge.
research: STUDENT RESEARCH GOES HERE: Premium tier analysis, pricing strategy, customer profiles.

name: Edgar's Cantina (Main Level)
summary: Main-level cantina named for Edgar Martinez. 21+ area with left-field views. Known for white queso nachos.

name: Hit It Here Cafe & Bar
summary: Full sit-down restaurant open to all ticket holders. Opens 90 min before game. Includes private event terrace (170+ guests).

name: Value Menu Stands
summary: Budget-friendly concession stands across the main concourse. All items under $5 including hot dogs, nachos, popcorn, peanuts.
research: STUDENT RESEARCH GOES HERE: Pricing strategy, traffic patterns, fan satisfaction.

name: Terrace Club & Loge Boxes
summary: Mid-tier premium with private lounge, for-purchase food/bev, in-seat wait service. Loge boxes include $100 F&B credit.

name: Edgar's Cantina (200 Level)
summary: Upper-level cantina. Same brand, different atmosphere and clientele than the main-level version.
research: STUDENT RESEARCH GOES HERE: Compare to main-level location.

name: Caught Looking Lounge
summary: Elevated lounge above The 'Pen. Access for 'Pen ticket holders. Includes Coors Light Rail Bar.

name: Press Club
summary: Newest premium tier. Replaced old press box. All-inclusive food/bev, full bar, oversized seating, private entrance, climate-controlled lounge.

name: All-Star Club
summary: Third-level premium with included buffet, cash bar, assigned plush seating, and excellent views.

name: Private Suites
summary: Private hospitality spaces with TVs, food service, skybridge entry, VIP parking. Capacity up to 52 guests.
research: STUDENT RESEARCH GOES HERE: Corporate/group hospitality analysis.

name: View Level Infield
summary: Upper-deck infield seats. Budget-friendly with panoramic field and Seattle skyline views.
research: STUDENT RESEARCH GOES HERE: Upper-deck fan experience, demographics.

name: View Level Concessions
summary: 300-level food including value menu, Holy Smoke BBQ (313), High Cheese Pizza (329). Different vendor mix than main level.
research: STUDENT RESEARCH GOES HERE: Compare food options across levels.
